subject: Astronomy & Space
title: Pulsars: Spinning Beacons
description: A micro-lesson that makes Pulsars: Spinning Beacons usable.

hook.text: Pulsars are neutron stars that emit beams of radiation from their poles. Each rotation produces a pulse.

buildup.text: They spin so regularly that early discoverers thought they'd found alien signals—calling them 'LGM' (Little Green Men).

discovery.text: The fastest known pulsar spins 716 times per second. Its surface moves at a quarter the speed of light.

twist.text: Pulsar timing is so precise that slight irregularities revealed the first exoplanets ever found—in 1992.

climax.text: Arrays of pulsars are being used to detect gravitational waves washing over the entire galaxy.

punchline.text: Dead stars spinning 716 times a second. Nature's most precise clocks.

quiz.question: Why were pulsars initially nicknamed 'LGM' (Little Green Men)?
options: Their incredibly regular pulses seemed like artificial alien signals
They emitted a green-colored light visible through telescopes
They were found near star systems thought to harbor aliens
